mnemonics.title: Elicitation Methods
mnemonics.content: I S A F O A: Interview, Survey, Observation, Focus Group, Observation, Affinity Diagramming

mnemonics.title: Requirement Types
mnemonics.content: FUN: Functional, Non-functional (U for Usability, N for all other qualities)

mnemonics.title: MoSCoW Priority
mnemonics.content: My Ship Carries Only Wares (Must, Should, Could, Won't)

mnemonics.title: Persona Elements
mnemonics.content: G E O: Goals, Environment, Obstacles

acronyms.expansion: Functionality, Usability, Reliability, Performance, Supportability + Constraints
Must have, Should have, Could have, Won't have

acronyms.usage: Design approach centered on user requirements
Measuring requirement success and system performance

visualTips.visualization: Draw as a horizontal timeline with user emotions shown as waves above/below the line, pain points as red markers
Create as a business card layout with photo, name, quote, demographics, goals, and frustrations in distinct sections
Use sticky notes on wall, grouped by color-coded themes radiating from central category
Stick figure actors on left, system as rectangle in center, arrows showing interactions connecting to system functions

analogies.analogy: Like an architect interviewing homeowners before designing a house - asking about needs, preferences, budget, and family size
Like character profiles in a movie script that help directors understand their audience
Like a recipe that lists ingredients (inputs), steps (process), and final dish (output)
Like a table of contents with page numbers linking chapters to content
Like packing a suitcase with limited space - must choose essential items first
